mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates month-over-month, signaling robust labor demand and consumer income expansion. This drives near-term equity valuations upward through increased earnings expectations and reduced recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2008 recovery phase with employment stabilization
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS month-over-month change versus 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll surprises fail to produce positive returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains elevated above 5% while inflation persists, indicating monetary policy restraint. This sustained tightening cycle compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2010 snapshot shows FEDFUNDS at 5.33% and CPIAUCSL at 314.121
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite elevated rates and inflation, or Spearman rank correlation between (FEDFUNDS + CPIAUCSL) composite and forward returns exceeds 0.15 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline sharply month-over-month, indicating weakening residential investment demand and forward-looking consumer confidence deterioration. This signals reduced near-term economic momentum and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: post-crisis demand recovery with housing volatility
failure_condition: Housing starts declines > 8% fail to precede negative 5-day returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC between HOUST momentum and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production accelerates above its 3-month trend, indicating strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side expansion. This signals improving corporate profitability and operational leverage, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-recession supply recovery with capacity expansion
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO growth and forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% while payroll employment stagnates, indicating labor market deterioration and forward-looking earnings risk. This dual signal of labor weakness compresses equity valuations through reduced consumer spending power and increased default risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 4.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < 50 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:March 2010 UNRATE at 4.2% and PAYEMS at 158779.0 thousand
regime_hint: labor market weakness with employment stagnation
failure_condition: Joint signal of rising unemployment and payroll stagnation fails to precede negative 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates below 0.3% month-over-month while housing starts remain flat, indicating synchronized weakness in consumer and residential demand. This dual demand contraction signals reduced near-term GDP growth and equity earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month change < 0.3% AND HOUST month-over-month change between -2% and +2%
evidence_refs: fact:March 2010 RSAFS at 709668.0 million dollars and HOUST at 1238.0 thousand units
regime_hint: demand-side weakness with synchronized consumer and housing stagnation
failure_condition: Synchronized retail and housing weakness fails to produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between RSAFS and HOUST momentum is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation measured by CPIAUCSL declines month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains above 5%, indicating real monetary tightening. This regime of rising real rates compresses equity valuations through increased cost of capital and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2010 CPIAUCSL at 314.121 and FEDFUNDS at 5.33%
failure_condition: Rising real rates fail to precede negative 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the real rate proxy (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL momentum) shows Rank IC below 0.05 with forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines month-over-month while payroll employment remains flat, indicating synchronized supply-side and labor market weakness. This dual contraction signals reduced corporate capacity utilization and earnings power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < 50 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:March 2010 INDPRO at 102.8887 and PAYEMS at 158779.0 thousand
regime_hint: supply-side contraction with labor market stagnation
failure_condition: Synchronized industrial production decline and payroll stagnation fail to produce negative 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between INDPRO and PAYEMS momentum is not statistically significant (p > 0.12).
